44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73

104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:

106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.

167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.

173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```

86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

73	/-
74	Planner strategy for `cechComplex_hom_identification`:
75	────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76	
77	Goal: construct a cochain-complex isomorphism
78	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`
79	where:
80	  • `K(𝒰)_p = ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
81	    (the degree-`p` term of `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`)
82	  • `Č^p(𝒰, F) = ∏_{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`
83	    (the degree-`p` term of `sectionCechComplex 𝒰 F`)
84	  Both complexes live in `Ab` after applying the global-sections functor.
85	
86	Step 1 — per-degree hom-coproduct duality:
87	  For each `p`, the Yoneda/free-presheaf adjunction gives
88	    `Hom(freeYoneda.obj V, F) ≅ F.obj V`  (natural in `V`).
89	  This is exactly `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` from `PresheafCech.lean`.
90	  Combined with the coproduct-hom identity
91	    `Hom(∐_σ A_σ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(A_σ, F)`
92	  (via `preadditiveYoneda` preservation of limits, or hand-rolled from
93	  `Limits.Sigma.desc` / `Limits.Sigma.ι`), we get a degree-wise `Ab`-iso
94	    `Hom(K(𝒰)_p, F) ≅ ∏_σ F.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) = Č^p(𝒰, F)`.
95	
96	Step 2 — differential intertwining:
97	  Show that the degree-wise isos commute with the differentials.  The differential on
98	  `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)` is precomposition with the Čech boundary `d_p : K(𝒰)_{p+1} → K(𝒰)_p`
99	  (the alternating sum of face maps of `cechFreeSimplicial`).  The differential on
100	  `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the alternating sum of restriction maps built by `sectionCechComplex`.
101	  These match under the per-degree iso by naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
102	
103	Step 3 — assemble:
104	  Use `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents` (or equivalent) to combine the per-degree
105	  isos into a full cochain-complex iso, supplying the naturality squares from Step 2.
106	
107	Key API:
108	  • `AlgebraicGeometry.freeYonedaHomAddEquiv` (PresheafCech.lean): the per-degree iso.
109	  • `Limits.Sigma.desc`, `Limits.Sigma.ι`: coproduct universal property in
110	    `X.PresheafOfModules`.
111	  • `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`: assemble component-wise isos into a
112	    full complex iso.
113	  • `sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean): the target complex.
114	  • `cechFreePresheafComplex` / `cechFreePresheafComplex_X` (FreePresheafComplex.lean):
115	    the source complex and its degreewise unfolding lemma.
116	-/

118	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech hom-identification -/
119	
120	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
121	
122	/-- **The hom cosimplicial abelian group `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)`.**
123	
124	The cosimplicial object in `Ab` obtained by applying the contravariant additive functor
125	`Hom_{PMod}(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` to the free Čech simplicial object
126	`cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰`.  Its value in degree `p` is the hom-group
127	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`, and its alternating-coface-map cochain complex is the left-hand
128	side of the Čech hom-identification.  Defining it this way makes the differential of the
129	hom-complex literally the alternating sum of `Hom(faceᵢ, F)`, so the identification with
130	`sectionCechComplex` reduces to a cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
131	
132	Project-local: Mathlib has neither `cechFreeSimplicial` nor this contravariant transport. -/
133	noncomputable def homCechCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
134	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} :=
135	  (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).rightOp ⋙ preadditiveYoneda.obj F
136	
137	/-- **The hom cochain complex `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F)`** — the left-hand side of the Čech
138	hom-identification.
139	
140	The alternating-coface-map cochain complex of `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F`. Built the same way as
141	`sectionCechComplex` (PresheafCech.lean) so that the planned identification
142	`cechComplex_hom_identification : homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F` is just
143	`(alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab).mapIso` of the cosimplicial natural isomorphism.
144	
145	Project-local: the hom-complex of the free Čech resolution has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
146	noncomputable def homCechComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
147	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
148	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F)
149	
150	/-- **Per-degree component of the Čech hom-identification.**
151	
152	For each simplicial degree `n`, the abelian-group isomorphism
153	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_n, F) ≅ ∏_σ F(⨅ₖ U(σ k))` assembled from (i) the coproduct–hom duality
154	`Hom(∐_σ Aσ, F) ≅ ∏_σ Hom(Aσ, F)` (the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` preserves the limit,
155	so `piComparison` is an iso, combined with `opCoproductIsoProduct`), and (ii) the per-index
156	free–Yoneda section identification `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`.
157	
158	Project-local: this is the degreewise core of `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`. -/
159	noncomputable def homCechSectionIsoApp (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
160	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) :
161	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).obj n ≅ (sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F).obj n :=
162	  (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapIso
163	      (opCoproductIsoProduct
164	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))
165	    ≪≫ asIso (piComparison (preadditiveYoneda.obj F)
166	        (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ => Opposite.op (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))))
167	    ≪≫ Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
168	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) F).toAddCommGrpIso)
169	
170	/-- `Pi.mapIso e` is by definition `Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom)`. -/
171	private lemma pi_mapIso_hom_eq {β : Type*} {C : Type*} [Category C] {f g : β → C}
172	    [HasProductsOfShape β C] (e : ∀ b, f b ≅ g b) :
173	    (Limits.Pi.mapIso e).hom = Limits.Pi.map (fun b => (e b).hom) := rfl
174	
175	/-- Characterizing property of `homCechSectionIsoApp`: its `σ`-component is precomposition
176	with the coproduct injection `Sigma.ι σ` followed by the free–Yoneda section identification.
177	Reduces the naturality of the assembled iso to the naturality of `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`. -/
178	private lemma homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
179	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (n : SimplexCategory) (σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
180	    (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F n).hom ≫
181	        Pi.π (fun τ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
182	          F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))) σ =
183	      (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map
184	          (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun τ : Fin (n.len + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
185	            freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ)) σ).op ≫
186	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom := by
187	  rw [homCechSectionIsoApp, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
188	    pi_mapIso_hom_eq]
189	  simp only [Category.assoc]
190	  erw [Pi.map_π, piComparison_comp_π_assoc]
191	  rw [← Category.assoc]
192	  congr 1
193	  exact ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
194	    (congrArg _ (opCoproductIsoProduct_hom_comp_π σ))
195	
196	/-- **Naturality of the free–Yoneda section identification in the open.**
197	
198	For an inclusion of opens `h : V ⟶ W`, precomposition with `freeYoneda.map h` on the hom-side
199	corresponds to restriction `F.presheaf.map h.op` on the section side.  This is the single
200	naturality square that powers the cosimplicial naturality of the Čech hom-identification. -/
201	private lemma freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality {V W : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X}
202	    (h : V ⟶ W) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
203	    (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map (freeYoneda.map h).op ≫
204	        (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv V F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom
205	      = (freeYonedaHomAddEquiv W F).toAddCommGrpIso.hom ≫ F.presheaf.map h.op := by
206	  ext ψ
207	  show freeYonedaHomEquiv V F (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ)
208	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op)) (freeYonedaHomEquiv W F ψ)
209	  show yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ))
210	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (F.presheaf.map h.op))
211	          (yonedaEquiv (PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ))
212	  rw [show PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv (freeYoneda.map h ≫ ψ)
213	        = yoneda.map h ≫ PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv ψ from
214	      (PresheafOfModules.freeAdjunction _).homEquiv_naturality_left (yoneda.map h) ψ,
215	    ← yonedaEquiv_naturality]
216	  rfl
217	
218	/-- **The cosimplicial natural isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.**
219	
220	Assembles the per-degree isomorphisms `homCechSectionIsoApp` into a natural isomorphism of
221	cosimplicial abelian groups. The naturality square reduces, via `homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π`
222	in both degrees, to the single naturality square `freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality` of the
223	free–Yoneda section identification.
224	
225	Project-local: the cosimplicial comparison of the hom-complex with the section complex has no
226	Mathlib counterpart. -/
227	noncomputable def homCechSectionCosimplicialIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
228	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
229	    homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechCosimplicial (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
230	  NatIso.ofComponents (homCechSectionIsoApp 𝒰 F) (by
231	    intro n m f
232	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
233	    intro σ
234	    dsimp only [sectionCechCosimplicial]
235	    rw [Category.assoc, Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π]
236	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F m σ]
237	    conv_rhs => rw [← Category.assoc]
238	    erw [homCechSectionIsoApp_hom_π 𝒰 F n (σ ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f))]
239	    erw [Category.assoc]
240	    erw [← freeYonedaHomAddEquiv_naturality
241	        (homOfLE (coverInterOpen_comp_le 𝒰 ⇑(SimplexCategory.Hom.toOrderHom f) σ)) F]
242	    dsimp only [homCechCosimplicial, Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map]
243	    erw [← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
244	    congr 1
245	    congr 1
246	    apply Quiver.Hom.unop_inj
247	    simp only [unop_comp]
248	    dsimp only [cechFreeSimplicial]
249	    erw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
250	    rfl
251	    )
252	
253	/-- **The Čech hom-identification** (blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`).
254	
255	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)` identifying the
256	hom-complex of the free Čech resolution with the section Čech complex of `F`. Obtained by
257	applying the alternating-coface-map complex functor to the cosimplicial natural isomorphism
258	`homCechSectionCosimplicialIso`, so the differential intertwining is automatic from the
259	cosimplicial naturality.
260	
261	Project-local: the comparison of the free-resolution hom-complex with the section complex has
262	no Mathlib counterpart. -/
263	noncomputable def cechComplex_hom_identification (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
264	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
265	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
266	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).mapIso (homCechSectionCosimplicialIso 𝒰 F)
267	
268	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — hom-complex as contravariant transport
269	
270	The injective-acyclicity assembly (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`, gated on Lane-1's
271	`cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`) needs `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` — the alternating *coface* complex of
272	the hom-cosimplicial object `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F` — to be identified with the
273	contravariant transport `Hom(-, F) = preadditiveYoneda.obj F` of the *opposite* of the free
274	Čech chain complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Once this identification is in hand,
275	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` (applied to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op`) and
276	`cechComplex_hom_identification` combine in a single step to turn the free resolution into
277	Čech acyclicity of injective sheaves.
278	
279	Project-local: the comparison of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped opposite
280	of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
281	
282	/-- The `i`-th coface of `homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F` is `Hom(-, F)` applied to the opposite of
283	the `i`-th face of `cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰`.  Holds definitionally (both unfold to
284	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).map (SimplexCategory.δ i).op).op`);
285	isolated as a `rfl` lemma to drive the differential identification `homCechComplex_d_eq`. -/
286	private lemma homCechCosimplicial_δ (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
287	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) {n : ℕ} (i : Fin (n + 2)) :
288	    (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F).δ i
289	      = (preadditiveYoneda.obj F).map ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i).op :=
290	  rfl
291	
292	/-- **Degreewise differential identification** of the hom-complex with the mapped opposite of
293	the free Čech complex.  Both differentials are alternating sums of (co)faces; pushing the
294	opposite and the additive functor `Hom(-, F)` through the alternating sum on the right and
295	using `homCechCosimplicial_δ` matches them term by term.  This is the naturality input for the
296	cochain-complex isomorphism `homCechComplexMapOpIso`. -/
297	private lemma homCechComplex_d_eq (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
298	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
299	    (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
300	      = (((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
301	          (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))).d p (p + 1) := by
302	  have hL : (homCechComplex 𝒰 F).d p (p + 1)
303	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (homCechCosimplicial 𝒰 F) p :=
304	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) p
305	  have hR : (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰).d (p + 1) p
306	      = AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰) p :=
307	    ChainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.d_squared _) p
308	  rw [hL, AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
309	    Functor.mapHomologicalComplex_obj_d, HomologicalComplex.op_d, hR,
310	    AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD]
311	  have hop := CategoryTheory.op_sum
312	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk (p + 1))))
313	    ((cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
314	    (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (p + 2)))
315	    (fun i => (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • (cechFreeSimplicial 𝒰).δ i)
316	  erw [hop, Functor.map_sum]
317	  refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
318	  rw [homCechCosimplicial_δ, CategoryTheory.op_zsmul]
319	  erw [Functor.map_zsmul]
320	  rfl
321	
322	/-- **Hom-complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
323	
324	The cochain-complex isomorphism `Hom(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Hom(-, F)((K(𝒰)_•)ᵒᵖ)` identifying the
325	alternating-coface hom-complex `homCechComplex 𝒰 F` with the contravariant transport
326	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex` of the opposite of the free Čech chain
327	complex `cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰`.  Degreewise the two complexes have the *same* term
328	(`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_p, F)`), so the components are identities; the differential squares are
329	`homCechComplex_d_eq`.
330	
331	This is the bridge that turns Lane-1's free resolution into Čech acyclicity: applying
332	`quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective` to `(cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰).op` produces a
333	quasi-isomorphism of mapped-opposite complexes, and this iso transports it onto
334	`homCechComplex 𝒰 F` (hence, via `cechComplex_hom_identification`, onto `sectionCechComplex`).
335	
336	Project-local: the identification of the alternating-coface hom-complex with the mapped
337	opposite of the alternating-face free complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
338	noncomputable def homCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
339	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
340	    homCechComplex 𝒰 F ≅
341	      ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
342	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰)) :=
343	  HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun _ => Iso.refl _) (by
344	    rintro i j (rfl : i + 1 = j)
345	    exact (Category.id_comp _).trans
346	      ((homCechComplex_d_eq 𝒰 F i).symm.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm))
347	
348	/-- **Section Čech complex as contravariant transport of the free Čech complex.**
349	
350	Composing `homCechComplexMapOpIso` with the Čech hom-identification
351	`cechComplex_hom_identification` gives a cochain-complex isomorphism between the mapped
352	opposite of the free Čech complex and the section Čech complex `Č•(coverOpen 𝒰, F)`.  This is
353	the single isomorphism needed for the injective-acyclicity assembly: once Lane 1 provides
354	`QuasiIso (cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰)`, mapping its opposite through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`
355	(quasi-iso by `quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective`) and transporting the source/target
356	across this iso identifies `sectionCechComplex` with the mapped opposite of a complex
357	concentrated in degree `0`, giving positive-degree vanishing.
358	
359	Project-local: assembled from two project-local isomorphisms. -/
360	noncomputable def sectionCechComplexMapOpIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
361	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
362	    ((preadditiveYoneda.obj F).mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj
363	        (HomologicalComplex.op (cechFreePresheafComplex 𝒰))
364	      ≅ sectionCechComplex (coverOpen 𝒰) F :=
365	  (homCechComplexMapOpIso 𝒰 F).symm ≪≫ cechComplex_hom_identification 𝒰 F
366	
367	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Hom(-, I)` is exact for injective `I`
368	
369	The presheaf-level Čech-acyclicity argument (`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`) needs the single
370	categorical fact that, for an injective object `I` of an abelian category, the contravariant
371	hom functor `Hom(-, I)` is exact — equivalently, the covariant functor
372	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab` preserves homology. Mathlib supplies the two pieces:
373	`preadditiveYoneda.obj I` always preserves finite limits (it is left exact), and injectivity
374	is equivalent to it preserving epimorphisms; we assemble these into preservation of finite
375	colimits (hence of homology), and feed the result through
376	`HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` to obtain quasi-isomorphism
377	preservation. These are stated for a general abelian category so they apply verbatim to
378	`X.PresheafOfModules` once the injectivity of `I` as a presheaf of modules is in hand.
379	
380	Project-local: Mathlib has the ingredients but not the packaged exactness of `Hom(-, I)` as a
381	homology-preserving functor. -/
382	
383	section InjectiveHomExact
384	
385	open CategoryTheory Limits
386	
387	variable {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
388	
389	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` preserves finite colimits for injective `I`.** For an injective object `I`
390	of an abelian category, the covariant functor `preadditiveYoneda.obj I : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab`
391	preserves finite colimits: it is left exact (preserves finite limits) automatically, and
392	injectivity makes it preserve epimorphisms, which together with left exactness gives
393	exactness. Combined with the automatic `PreservesFiniteLimits` instance this yields
394	`(preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesHomology` by `Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact`.
395	
396	Project-local: the exactness of `Hom(-, I)` for injective `I` is the categorical engine of
397	`lem:injective_cech_acyclic`. -/
398	instance preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective
399	    (I : C) [Injective I] : PreservesFiniteColimits (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) := by
400	  have hepi : (preadditiveYoneda.obj I).PreservesEpimorphisms :=
401	    (Injective.injective_iff_preservesEpimorphisms_preadditiveYoneda_obj I).mp inferInstance
402	  rw [Functor.preservesFiniteColimits_iff_forall_exact_map_and_epi]
403	  intro S hS
404	  have hg := hS.epi_g
405	  exact ⟨hS.exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel _ hS.mono_f inferInstance,
406	    hepi.preserves S.g⟩
407	
408	/-- **`Hom(-, I)` carries quasi-isomorphisms to quasi-isomorphisms for injective `I`.**
409	Since `preadditiveYoneda.obj I` preserves homology
410	(`preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective`), applying it degreewise to a
411	quasi-isomorphism `φ` of chain complexes in `Cᵒᵖ` produces a quasi-isomorphism of the
412	`Hom`-cochain complexes. This is the form consumed by `injective_cech_acyclic`: the
413	augmentation `cechFreeComplexAug 𝒰` (a quasi-isomorphism once Lane 1 lands), taken opposite
414	and mapped through `preadditiveYoneda.obj F`, stays a quasi-isomorphism.
415	
416	Project-local: packages `quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology` against the injective-hom
417	exactness instance above. -/
418	lemma quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective (I : C) [Injective I]
419	    {ι : Type*} {c : ComplexShape ι} {K L : HomologicalComplex Cᵒᵖ c}
420	    (φ : K ⟶ L) [QuasiIso φ] :
421	    QuasiIso (((preadditiveYoneda.obj I).mapHomologicalComplex c).map φ) := by
422	  rw [quasiIso_iff]
423	  intro i
424	  exact HomologicalComplex.quasiIsoAt_map_of_preservesHomology φ (preadditiveYoneda.obj I) i
425	    (hφ := QuasiIso.quasiIsoAt (f := φ) i)
426	
427	end InjectiveHomExact

429	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Čech `H¹` vanishing ⟹ cocycles are coboundaries
430	
431	The surjectivity-on-sections step `ses_cech_h1` (blueprint `lem:ses_cech_h1`, Stacks
432	`lemma-ses-cech-h1`) consumes the {\v C}ech-algebra fact that a covering with
433	`Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0` has *every* {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle equal to a coboundary. This is the
434	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` content of that lemma, isolated here as a self-contained
435	statement about the section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (no sheaf gluing or
436	local-surjectivity input). It is the converse direction of
437	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` (CechAcyclic), packaged in section
438	coordinates via `sectionCechProductEquiv` / `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
439	
440	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules, so
441	neither the homology-to-exactness reduction nor its coordinate form exist there. -/

443	section CechH1Coboundary
444	
445	open AlgebraicTopology
446	
447	/-- **Homology vanishing ⟹ coface-differential exactness** (converse of
448	`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`). If the degree-`(q+1)` homology of the
449	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, the underlying group homomorphisms of the two
450	consecutive coface differentials `objD q`, `objD (q+1)` form an exact sequence. Pure
451	homological algebra: the same `exactAt_iff_isZero_homology` / `exactAt_iff'` /
452	`ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact` chain, run in the extraction direction.
453	
454	Project-local: extraction form of the `Ab`-side homology bridge of
455	`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`, needed by the `ses_cech_h1` coboundary step. -/
456	theorem sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
457	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (q : ℕ)
458	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology (q + 1))) :
459	    Function.Exact
460	      (ConcreteCategory.hom (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q))
461	      (ConcreteCategory.hom
462	        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1))) := by
463	  have hf : (sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)
464	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) q :=
465	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) q
466	  have hg : (sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2)
467	      = AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) (q + 1) :=
468	    CochainComplex.of_d _ _ (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.d_squared _) (q + 1)
469	  have key : Function.Exact
470	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d q (q + 1)))
471	      (ConcreteCategory.hom ((sectionCechComplex U F).d (q + 1) (q + 2))) := by
472	    have hh := h
473	    rw [← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff_isZero_homology,
474	        (sectionCechComplex U F).exactAt_iff' q (q + 1) (q + 2) (by simp) (by simp),
475	        ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hh
476	    exact hh
477	  rw [hf, hg] at key
478	  exact key
479	
480	/-- **Čech `1`-cocycles are coboundaries when `Ȟ¹(𝒰, F) = 0`** — the
481	`\uses{def:cech_complex}` heart of `ses_cech_h1`.
482	
483	Working in the section coordinates of `sectionCechCosimplicial` (a family of sections
484	`c σ ∈ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` indexed by double-indices `σ : Fin 2 → ι`), if the family
485	satisfies the {\v C}ech `1`-cocycle identity (the alternating sum of its restrictions
486	over each triple-index `σ : Fin 3 → ι` vanishes) and the degree-`1` homology of the
487	section {\v C}ech complex vanishes, then `c` is a coboundary: there is a `0`-cochain
488	`t` (sections `t σ ∈ F(U (σ 0))` over single-indices) with `c σ` the alternating sum
489	of the face restrictions of `t`, i.e. `c σ = t_{σ 1}|_{σ} - t_{σ 0}|_{σ}` once the
490	two-term sum is expanded. The restriction maps are `sectionCechFaceRestr`, exactly the
491	{\v C}ech coface restrictions appearing in `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
492	
493	Project-local: the section-coordinate coboundary extraction for the section {\v C}ech
494	complex has no Mathlib counterpart. -/
495	theorem sectionCech_one_coboundary_of_isZero_homology {ι : Type u}
496	    (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
497	    (h : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U F).homology 1))
498	    (c : ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
499	    (hcoc : ∀ σ : Fin 3 → ι,
500	      ∑ i : Fin 3, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
501	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
502	          (c (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) = 0) :
503	    ∃ t : ∀ σ : Fin 1 → ι, F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))),
504	      ∀ σ : Fin 2 → ι, c σ = ∑ i : Fin 2, (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •
505	        ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr U F σ i)
506	          (t (σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom)) := by
507	  have he := sectionCech_objD_exact_of_isZero_homology U F 0 h
508	  set c' : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk 1)) :=
509	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).symm c with hc'
510	  have hpe1 : sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1 c' = c :=
511	    (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 1).apply_symm_apply c
512	  have hzero : ConcreteCategory.hom
513	      (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial U F) 1) c' = 0 := by
514	    apply (sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2).injective
515	    funext σ
516	    rw [sectionCech_objD_apply U F 1 c' σ, hpe1]
517	    rw [show sectionCechProductEquiv U F 2 0 σ = 0 from by
518	      rw [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply]; exact map_zero _]
519	    exact hcoc σ
520	  obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := (he c').mp hzero
521	  refine ⟨sectionCechProductEquiv U F 0 b, fun σ => ?_⟩
522	  rw [← hpe1, ← hb, sectionCech_objD_apply U F 0 b σ]
523	
524	end CechH1Coboundary
525	
526	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
10	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
11	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
12	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},

17	\section{Setup and motivation}
18	
19	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
20	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
21	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
22	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
23	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
24	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
25	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
26	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
27	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
28	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
29	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
30	
31	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
32	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
33	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
34	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
35	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
36	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
37	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
38	\[
39	  \begin{array}{ccc}
40	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
41	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
42	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
43	  \end{array}
44	\]
45	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
46	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
47	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
48	
49	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
50	
51	\begin{definition}
52	
53	\leanok
54	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
55	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
56	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
57	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor, def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
58	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
59	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
60	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
61	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
62	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
63	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
64	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
65	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
66	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
67	  % the unit ideal, see
68	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
69	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
70	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
71	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
72	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
73	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
74	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
75	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
76	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
77	  \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in
78	  cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
79	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
80	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
81	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
82	  \[
83	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
84	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
85	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
86	  \]
87	  with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and
88	  codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree
89	  \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric
90	  nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod_i U_i\) over \(X\),
91	  introduced in \S\ref{sec:cech_three_part} below) is a \emph{simplicial} object
92	  in \emph{schemes}, but applying the covariant direct-image functor turns it into
93	  a \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); consequently the
94	  associated alternating-coface complex is a \emph{cochain} complex (differentials
95	  raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection
96	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
97	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
98	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
99	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
100	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
101	\end{definition}

167	\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
168	\label{sec:cech_three_part}
169	
170	It is convenient to factor the construction of
171	Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
172	pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the sole non-trivial
173	mathematical content is isolated in the middle piece.
174	
175	\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
176	Package the affine cover as a single morphism
177	\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
178	coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
179	{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
180	\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
181	\[
182	  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
183	    \;=\;
184	    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
185	      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
186	\]
187	with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
188	subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
189	is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
190	\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
191	and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
192	arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
193	spaces over which the construction is indexed.
194	
195	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
196	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
197	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
198	\[
199	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
200	  \qquad
201	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
202	\]
203	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
204	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
205	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
206	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
207	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
208	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
209	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
210	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
211	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
212	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
213	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
214	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
215	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
216	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
217	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
218	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. Concretely,
219	pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
220	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
221	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
222	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
223	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
224	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts: the mate identity
225	expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback comparison as the pushforward
226	comparison, its normalisation at the identity morphism, and the pseudofunctor
227	unitor and pentagon (associativity) coherences. The laws are nonetheless not formal
228	trivialities: each is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison
229	isomorphisms along the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and
230	past the adjunction unit, transporting along the over-triangle equality; the
231	obstacle is the bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
232	
233	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
234	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
235	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
236	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
237	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
238	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
239	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
240	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
241	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
242	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
243	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
244	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
245	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
246	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
247	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
248	that content is the pseudofunctor coherence of pushforward and pullback: the
249	comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
250	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
251	and their unitor/pentagon identities.
252	
253	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
254	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
255	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
256	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
257	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
258

264	  \emph{cover arrow} is the single morphism
265	  \(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of
266	  the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging
267	  the cover as one arrow lets the generic {\v C}ech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery

306	  over-triangle \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\); the contravariant functor produces the
307	  restriction comparison \(G(Y_1, p_1) \to G(Y_2, p_2)\) as the five-step composite
308	  \[
309	    p_{1*}\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
310	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{1*}(\eta)\,}
311	    p_{1*}\,\bar g_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
312	      \xrightarrow{\,\sim\,}

314	      \;=\;

324	  substitutions are realised as coercions along \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\).

338	  Rewrite the head \(p_*(\eta)\) followed by the pushforward comparison as the

347	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_pentagon}

351	  \[

363	  The composition identity \(G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g)\) follows from three

369	  isomorphism \((p \circ q \circ r)^* \cong r^* q^* p^*\). Third, the over-triangle transport

485	\begin{definition}
486	[Standard affine cover from a spanning family]
487	  \label{def:standard_affine_cover}
488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}
489	  \mathlibok
490	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open}).}
491	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
492	  \mathcal{O})\)) and let \(s : \iota \to R\) be a family of elements that generate
493	  the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\). The
494	  \emph{standard affine open cover} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) associated with
495	  \(s\) is the affine open cover whose index set is \(\iota\), whose \(i\)-th member
496	  is the basic open \(D(s_i)\), and whose \(i\)-th structure morphism is the map of
497	  affine schemes induced by the localisation homomorphism \(R \to R_{s_i}\); that
498	  is, in Mathlib notation the defining equation
499	  \(\bigl(\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\bigr)_f\, i =
500	  \operatorname{Spec.map}\bigl(\text{algebraMap}\; R\; (\operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i))\bigr)\),
501	  so that the sections over the \(i\)-th piece are the \emph{away} localisation
502	  \(R_{s_i} = \operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i)\) and the geometric piece is
503	  \(D(s_i)\). This is exactly the standard open covering
504	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i D(s_i)\) by basic opens, packaged as a single
505	  cover object. It is the spanning-family bundle \((s, hs)\) that the affine
506	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is stated over.
507	\end{definition}

509	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
510	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
511	
512	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
513	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
514	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
515	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
516	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
517	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
518	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
519	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
520	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
521	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
522	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
523	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
524	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
525	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
526	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.

670	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
671	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
672	  \[
673	    D^\bullet : \quad
674	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
675	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
676	    \cdots,

684	    \;\Longrightarrow\;
685	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
686	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),

690	  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group

693	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.

696	  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
697	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
698	  product}

700	  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
701	  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
702	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
703	  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
704	  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
705	  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
706	  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
707	  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
708	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
709	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
710	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
711	  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence

713	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)

719	  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
720	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then

722	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), for which the per-coordinate comparisons are
723	  immediate from \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\); a general
724	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case via the affine equivalence

730	\begin{proof}
731	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
732	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
733	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
734	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
735	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
736	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
737	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
738	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
739	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
740	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
741	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
742	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
743	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) (the coface match of
744	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). The homology vanishing then follows in
745	  three steps. First, \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) turns
746	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology at \(p\) into \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
747	  the short complex at \(p\). Second, because the degree-\(p\) objects are
748	  \emph{categorical products in \(\mathrm{Ab}\)} rather than \(R\)-modules, the abelian
749	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
750	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) --- not the module-category criterion ---
751	  reduces \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) to the underlying-group exactness
752	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\). Third, the
753	  element-level product equivalence
754	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
755	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
756	  identifies each product group with the per-\(\sigma\) localised module \(D^p\), so the
757	  group-level exactness is exactly \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to
758	  D^{p+1})\), supplied by \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
759	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}).
760	
761	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
762	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
763	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
764	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
765	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
766	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
767	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
768	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
769	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
770	  being proved.
771	\end{proof}
